summarization.a: A reaction scheme illustrating the synthesis of Sn1-xGexOy or Zn1-xGexOy Electron Selective Layer (ESL) by ALD
summarization.b: A line chart showing the optical absorption of Ge doped electron selective layer as a function of energy
summarization.c: A line chart depicting the solar cell efficiency of Sn1-xGexOy and Zn1-xGexOy as a function of Ge content of ESL.

data_extraction.a:
| Process Step                          | Description                                      |
|--------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------|
| Precursors                           | Ge(N(CH₃)₂)₄ and Sn(N(CH₃)₂)₄ or Zn(C₂H₅)₂      |
| Reactant                             | H₂O                                             |
| Deposited Layer                      | Sn₁₋ₓGeₓOᵧ or Zn₁₋ₓGeₓOᵧ (Electron Selective Layer, ESL) |
| Substrate                            | (Ag, Cu)(In, Ga)Se₂                             |
| Method                               | Atomic Layer Deposition (ALD) Supercycle        |
data_extraction.b:
| Ge Content in ESL | Optical Absorption Shift |
|--------------------|---------------------------|
| Low               | Lower absorption edge     |
| High              | Higher absorption edge    |
data_extraction.c:
| Ge Content in ESL | Sn₁₋ₓGeₓOᵧ Efficiency | Zn₁₋ₓGeₓOᵧ Efficiency |
|--------------------|--------------------------|--------------------------|
| Low               | Moderate                | Low                      |
| Medium            | Peak                    | Increasing               |
| High              | Decline                 | Peak                     |

a.question: How is the ternary Ge based materials developed by ALD?
a.answer:
In the ALD supercycle approach, the GeOy ALD cycle was interchanged with either ZnO or SnOy cycles to deposit TGO and ZGO with varying conduction
band positions (Ec), respectively

b.question: Does Ge content change the optical absorption?

c.question: How is the ALD based ESL made compatible for solar cell application?
c.answer: To ensure (Ag,Cu)(In,Ga)Se2 (ACIGS) solar cell compatibility, a 120 °C ALD process was developed for GeOy using Ge(N(CH3)2)4 and H2O as precursors.

c.question: How did the ALD based ESL fare in device performance?
c.answer: The open-circuit voltage initially increased as the Ge content of the TGO and ZGO films increased due to the ESL Ec simultaneously shifting up from the low position in ZnO or SnOy to match the ACIGS Ec. As the Ge content increased further, the fill factor (FF) of these devices decreased since the ESL Ec became positioned significantly above the ACIGS Ec, forming an energy barrier as seen from ACIGS. As a result, the efficiency of the ACIGS solar cell peaked for an intermediate Ge content for both TGO and ZGO
